name: Johnny Mathis
abstract: John Royce Mathis (born September 30, 1935) is an American singer. Starting his career with singles of standard music, Mathis is one of the best-selling recording artists of the 20th century.

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: he was writing for Johnny Mathis -- in fact, that's his arranger on 'Chances Are,' which people are still playing and was a big hit for Johnny Mathis.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...these were the people that basically were before the Billy Eckstein's and the Lou Rawls and the Johnny Mathis and the Sammy Davis's.

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Pallo
about_quotes.evidence: if you listen to Johnny Mathis you’ll hear the first thump... that was Tony Mottola playing rhythm.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Pallo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Putter Smith
about_quotes.evidence: I mean stuff like Johnny Mathis and Tony Bennett quality, Frank Sinatra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Putter Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 27, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Conniff
about_quotes.evidence: And I did the early Johnny Mathis’ “Chances Are” it’s not for me to say, with the piano hook.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Conniff interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: That’s one. Yeah. And then there’s another one that I whistled on for Johnny Mathis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]
